author: @extragamer2935
text:
In my opinion decentralization is best when you have multiple independent points of draw, but the trade off is increasing points of failure. If you want to midagate a total system failure,from a single failure point, wire each coil continuously to battery bank that's wired in parallel. Terminated by charging controler. You can see a physical wire diagram at your local parts store, this is how car batteries are charged.
?

author: @nigeljohnson9820
text:
Dumpster diving seems to be an interesting activity for engineers from all around the world, who work on industrial estates. It is  amazing the stuff that companies throw away. 
You might like to take a look at the  Australia YouTube channel, EEV blog and EEV blog2, assuming you have not seen it before. The latter channel is of particular relevance, as it sometime covers the electronic waste to be found in the communal dumpster area. It is a real horn of plenty.
I don't thing engineers willingly throw anything away, its the company bean counters who insist of disposing of perfectly good and working equipment.

author: @martinwinfield2935
text: These companys are led by accountants who see stock not moving as no money coming in. What they do not seem to understand that they pass these losses onto there customers. They are often big PLCs whom have the same approach and will pay as they will only deal with these suppliers. I spent years trying to convice them thay could save money by buying from smaller outfits. They just will not do it. A lot of words to really say great for you to get this stuff, but what a waste for the things that end up in the skip.

author: @snaz27
text:
I made a Ferrari for £20, I had a mate who had some genuine Ferrari wheels knocking around and happily gave them to me. Then I was walking down the street and I noticed an old Ferrari chassis sat on a drive, the homeowner was outside and I asked about it and they said I can take it away.

Then I found an engine somewhere and some other bits and bobs... I bought a Ferrari badge on eBay for £20 and there we are, a Ferrari for £20... 🤣🤣🤣

Quite simple really... 😜

author: @Retinalism
text: The multiple, small, redundant units concept is at the heart of the TESLA battery system so many of us now mimic. This is actually a very good find in more than the material sense alone. The fact that it got you to ask that perennial question “what can I do with that” (otherwise unrealised VALUE of the discarded materials) and then digress your development thinking from simply generating to, now, storing energy (so critical to successful “renewable” energy systems) is a tribute to the process of “free learning” through discovery, rather than structured, syllabus based direction. Your vids are always top notch and inspirational, and remind me just a little of Prof Julius Sumner-Miller approach, just with a practical bent.

text: Now this is odd I mentioned an idea like this and you’ve said that idea on this before I made the comment lol to use batteries per coil something I’ve always thought of doing .... well my idea was different though it was to use capacitors between the coils accumulating between each phase to increase the efficiency of the alternator the idea linked to this was to wire a separate discharge terminal that connected to a motor that was triggered by a magnetic reed switch to a drive motor when I was thinking a while ago about a perpetual motor generator lol ....I think there’s scope to create something different here one interesting thing Was Mr Teslaonian  was making a kelvin water dropper and found a single pole magnet could be made from this effect which could have a lot of applications..... also another idea if you choose to make your own stator a big one with your own wire you could heat shrink screws in a pattern on the the board and use them as pegs to wrap the enamelled copper love these videos 👍

author: @jasonwitt8619
text: Do me a big favore mate, could you show me how to use and wire the ballast in the way you are using it? I have a bunch of those ballast that I got from a company rebuilding it's interior. They gave me all of their old fluorescent lights and each had a big ballast. The problem is I have never messed with the ballast before and the ones I have are 3 wires and I have never tried to use one. I had to cut the wires to get them out because the wire was hidden within each light between metal. I have no idea how to use them but I knew they were important when I got them, LOL. It was the only reason I got those lights, was to get those ballast out to be used on a rainy day. Also can you show if there is a certain direction of current that they have to be connected in, with direction or not with the batteries, I also plan to use mine with a supercap to give power to a motor or am I correct in saying the ballast is a kind of supercap? Thanks mate

replies.author: @jasonwitt8619
replies.text: I got about 15 of them sitting in a box. They all were hooked up to 110v before being taken down. They are a little larger in size than the one you had in this video but my guess is that the ballast is a large supercap that keeps the light solid and not flicker but I'm not sure and this is why I'm asking you the pro, someone I trust.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: send me the part number and manufacturer mate so I know what it is - because all a ballast is is really a big inductor

replies.author: @jasonwitt8619
replies.text: I also have another type by ACCUPRO model No. AP-RC-232IP-120-1, This one is a lot smaller in size and weight than the other I gave you. The other one is a big boy and was hooked to 4 bulbs at a time.

author: @cptrikester2671
text:
If one is not fortunate enough to find golden garbage (batteries), 
Could MW capacitors (or others) be used as energy storage?

replies.author: @ronaldd2154
replies.text:
Microwave caps? I'd say not really as its not intended for this type of usage and would highly advise against it. You can, but I don't think it would last long for this application as they are oil filled and can easily superheat (causing leaks, explosions) , plus they are thousands of volts and very low capacity (you would need a lot, and I mean a lot) , plus not exactly fun to get a shock from, or accidentally creating a" human circuit" which can stop your heart from beating, or causing palpitations, not to mention you would then need a transformer to convert the 1000s of volts down to a lower usable voltage. 

Caps in general you will need a much, much larger bank taking up more size as compared to chemical based batteries like say lead acid. On the plus, would allow for a near instantaneous charge (a few seconds) , provided you have enough energy /amps captured from your device/turbine. Caps atm is just not the way, but in the future with graphene based cells, they allow for much higher capacities/energy storage vs current sizes. Tbh, MW caps is not worth it, but if you'd like to experiment then do so with A LOT of caution and research it thoroughly before attempting even touching it. These caps can self recharge! (FREE ENERGY 😂⚡/wirelessly. Electromagnetic rays can slowly, but eventually recharge it due to something called permissivity) So do so at your own risk. If you don't know much about it, don't play around with it. 

Ps, the magnetrons ceramic part contains beryllium oxide, and if crushed or chipped, sanded, scraped, can release dust particles into the air. If you breath them in, you could get berylliosis /lung cancer. I really hope Rob puts up warning signs on his vids and spreads the words on this before the general public start getting any ideas. ✌️

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: you could mate but they won't store much - they are normally rated to 2kV or so that doesn't mean that's what they store - it's the max voltage they can go up to - they will charge to whatever voltage you are outputting - and in this case it is 30V or so - so that is what they will have on them and as they are a few microfarads it won't be a ton of energy so you would need a lot!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: they used to have beryllium oxide - but they don't anymore - if they are pink they have beryllium oxide in them but it has been many years since that was the case

author: @kilokilos
text: A washing machine pump has a magnetic armature running in a lamination with a coil at the bottom I found that if you put a neodymium magnet on the lamination it seems the power generated when turned might go up by quite a lot. Connected to a voltage doubler circuit, some capacitors and diodes it easily gets up to 120 volts. Enough to power a solar regulator. This is by far the most effective device I ever tested. Maybe the armature can be removed and the coil and be put under the magnets lamination side up? Worth a try.

author: @michaelpearson5465
text:
Brilliant this is great stuff, I think in general renewable energy is taken for granted in a sence, because yes they generate "clean" energy, meaning that they produce no greenhouse gasses when they are running. However, I feel that not enough emphasis is given to the fact that the manufacturing of these renewables is still no where near carbon neutral.

For us to succeed in reducing our carbon emissions not only do we need to work on renewable infrastructure, but equally at how we can make the manufacturing more efficient and have less of an impact.

As you have clearly shown it is possible, we are a through away society, but we don't realise we are throwing away valuable resources. To me its a no brainer, why waste time and resources digging more resources out of the ground when its already at our fingertips.

author: @hubrisnaut
text: It is amazing what get's thrown out. I once considered starting a business tearing down and selling parts from discarded items. I had a build project for my business (a continuous throughput rotary vegetable washer), and a slim budget so I went to a large "recycling center" to look for parts a number of times (found them ...). The things thrown out would blow your mind. I picked up things like a perfectly fine propane camp stove, a perfectly fine running snow blower! etc... The highlight was one day a woman backs her truck up and unloads 3 fairly large tool boxes filled with tools; screwdrivers, wrenches (spanners), ratchets and sockets, etc.. ! I inquired into why she was throwing them away. Turns out, she had gotten divorced and was "getting rid" of her ex-husband's "stuff"... The tool boxes went into the back of my truck that day... Now, I did have to pay the recycling center for what I took. They charged me based on the scrap value of steel. Which I think was $100 (US) a ton!, if I remember correctly..

I wish that PV cell panel designers would do it that way.

author: @aaknuu
text: How does a rotating magnetic field affect the batteries.

replies.author: @ronaldd2154
replies.text: Good question. Heating and perhaps interference of chemical reaction towards susceptible elements (metals). If batteries are stacked directly below the iron-core coil I'd imagine it wouldn't have any concerning amount of magnetic influence/Flux fields. By having a small gap/distance between battery and coil via 3-5 mm cardboard will basically shield it. Perhaps having the batteries more towards the middle away from magnets?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: not at all mate - you need a very strong magnetic field to start interfering with chemical reactions

author: @ktm42080

author: @imbabyface
text:
This goes to my point of setting up multiple devices on the One Turbine.  As my suggestion on a recent previous video of adding additional transformers on the one spinning turbine setup with the out ring of attached magnets.

Great idea Mister!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Great tip too mate - cheers

replies.author: @imbabyface
replies.text:
@ThinkingandTinkering If the additional Units are not reducing the Turbine speed, and there is an identical area opposite the Lower Banks of magnets and Transformers, why not double the impressive Energy Output to exceed the 1Kilo watt Generator?

It would be So Fantastic to see this double output Vertical Turbine Generator charging A SETUP of your best Self made storage Batteries. This would be a very efficient System for " Off The Grid Living."   

Making a number of these Generators depending on your Electrical Utility Company.

Some Utility Company like Hydro One in Ontario actually buy excess energy that you can produce and sell that excess Power to the Electrical Utility Company customers during peek times.

They buy excess energy cheaper from you than the price  they pay buying additional power from other Utility Companies when the demand exceeds their supply.

So not only can you generate your own Power but also turn this into a Source of Income.   $$$

Damn Cool I think!

author: @tinkmarshino
text: Do we now trade simplicity and ease of build and change it to a more efficient and complicated way because you found something thrown away because you have a shop next to an electronics place? So far I can get everything you have mentioned in my little town.. But the odds of me finding 20 (or even one) bank of small batteries like those you have there or more than a couple of old ballasts ( and I am not sure if there is a difference in the old ones and the new one) is not going to happen. Are we now going to change form a project that anyone in any small town around the world can do to a more complicated one? As for large batter vs small.. which is easier to find a car battery or a bunch of small rechargeable nimh batteries? Around here you would be hard pressed to find those little things.. not impossible but very hard where as an old car batter you can find most easily. Lets stay with the original build.. something that every one can find. In the big cities it may be a throw away society but in the smaller towns it is not.. Just an opinion.. you must do what you think is right..

replies.author: @Teknopottu
replies.text: Wise point. But I would be very exited too if I was to find something like this. We'll see how he continues.

replies.author: @tinkmarshino
replies.text: @Teknopottu no doubt.. but I am old and have used up most of my luck by now ;-) But it would be something to find that stuff.. I would start dumpster diving but if I got in I would not be able to get out!! oh well. I have fun watching you kids come up with all of these very cool things that we (my generation) wish we could have done.. Carry on son!

replies.author: @ronaldd2154
replies.text:
I agree, though Rob has given us a generic formula/ idea to follow. Some parts we can copy like the PVC blades, but those smart Boards I cant get hold of unless lucky, or purchased second hand for hundreds of dollars. But I found something else which is more affordable and very durable, and more likely than not available at your local hardware store, > Aluminium composite board. 

We can use any batteries, but will have to accommodate in different ways to make it work, so this part will require asking questions. Youtube is full of information regarding batteries and energy storage/charging. My way will be using old, reconditioned lead acid, 12V car batteries. There are also plenty of Aluminium pop cans littered around, im sure theres a way to create a rechargeable battery with it, in fact im sure Rob would have a idea for it 💡🤔👍.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: there are options mate - you could recycle laptop batteries for example - but I get your point and I am thinking of saving this idea for a future upgrade and sticking with the original plane - even so I like to point out that the design is flexible if you want it to bo - hmm - I might do a video on just that

replies.author: @tinkmarshino
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering  Most of the lap top battery's I could find look like pillows and do not hold a charge any more.. and even those are hard to find out here.. Thanks for all you do Rob.. you have great idea's no matter what! and I can find what ever I need eventually so carry on.. I will follow..

replies.author: @aleksanderlikar5375

The proverb says "In seven years everything will be useful"
And then we have no pile of junk in, garage, basement, and attic.
We don’t know what times are coming and you can’t make anything out of nothing.

replies.author: @ronaldd2154
replies.text:
@aleksanderlikar5375  well said. "They" are transitioning towards renewable energy, rainbows and unicorns at an alarming rate. Even prince charles put his foot down on global warming. Big oil moving towards "green", electric cars, electric everything. Question that requires begging.... WHY???! telling us what we want to hear? Updating the entire system, converting it to electrical / digital......easier control. I can't help but feel the rotten core of system is still there, only desguising itself, taking us for another ride.... 

Im maybe wrong though and perhaps should have some faith in humanity. Perhaps the times are changing for better, with better standards for all.
